questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Awaara released?
questions.explanation: Awaara was released in 1951.

questions.q: Who directed the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Awaara was directed by Raj Kapoor under his R.K. Films banner.

questions.q: Who played the male lead, the tramp Raj, in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor both directed Awaara and starred as the tramp-like protagonist Raj.

questions.q: Which actress played the female lead Rita opposite Raj Kapoor in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Nargis played Rita, continuing her celebrated on-screen pairing with Raj Kapoor.

questions.q: What is the famous title song of the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: The film's signature song is 'Awaara Hoon', sung by Mukesh.

questions.q: Which playback singer gave voice to Raj Kapoor for the title song 'Awaara Hoon' in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Mukesh, long regarded as Raj Kapoor's musical 'voice', sang 'Awaara Hoon'.

questions.q: Under which film banner was the 1951 film Awaara produced?
questions.explanation: Awaara was produced under Raj Kapoor's own studio, R.K. Films.

questions.q: The central theme of the 1951 film Awaara is best described as a debate between which two forces?
questions.explanation: Awaara dramatises the nature-versus-nurture question, asking whether heredity or environment shapes a man's character.

questions.q: In the 1951 film Awaara, the protagonist Raj is the son of a man holding which profession?
questions.explanation: Raj is the son of a respected judge, yet grows up among thieves on the streets.

questions.q: In which language was the 1951 film Awaara primarily made?
questions.explanation: Awaara was made in Hindi-Urdu, the standard language of mainstream Bombay cinema.

questions.q: Which music director duo composed the songs for the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: The score and songs of Awaara were composed by the duo Shankar-Jaikishan.

questions.q: What is the name of the elaborate dream sequence song in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: The film's celebrated fantasy dream sequence is set to 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi'.

questions.q: Which veteran actor, Raj Kapoor's real-life father, played the judge Raghunath in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Kapoor, Raj Kapoor's father, played the stern judge Raghunath, mirroring the on-screen father-son relationship.

questions.q: Who played the menacing bandit Jagga, who corrupts the young Raj in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: K.N. Singh portrayed the vengeful bandit Jagga who deliberately drags Raj into a life of crime.

questions.q: The 1951 film Awaara became an exceptional commercial and cultural success in which country, where its title song was widely sung?
questions.explanation: Awaara was a massive hit in the Soviet Union, where 'Awaara Hoon' became enormously popular.

questions.q: Who wrote the screenplay and story for the 1951 film Awaara, also a frequent collaborator of Raj Kapoor?
questions.explanation: Khwaja Ahmad Abbas wrote Awaara, beginning his long association with Raj Kapoor's socially themed films.

questions.q: The on-screen persona Raj Kapoor cultivated in Awaara is widely compared to which Hollywood comedian's famous character?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor's lovable vagabond image in Awaara was modelled on Charlie Chaplin's Tramp.

questions.q: Besides Awaara, which other Raj Kapoor and Nargis film helped cement them as one of Hindi cinema's most iconic pairs?
questions.explanation: Shree 420 (1955) was another landmark Raj Kapoor-Nargis collaboration alongside Awaara.

questions.q: In the 1951 film Awaara, the character Rita, played by Nargis, works in which profession?
questions.explanation: Rita is a lawyer who ends up defending Raj in court during the film's climax.

questions.q: The climax of the 1951 film Awaara is built around a dramatic sequence set in which location?
questions.explanation: The film culminates in a courtroom trial where Raj is defended by Rita and confronted by his estranged father.

questions.q: The 1951 film Awaara was nominated for the Grand Prize at which major international film festival in 1953?
questions.explanation: Awaara competed for the Grand Prize at the 1953 Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for the 1951 film Awaara, noted for its striking dream-sequence visuals?
questions.explanation: Radhu Karmakar was the cinematographer of Awaara and a longtime collaborator of Raj Kapoor at R.K. Films.

questions.q: Which two lyricists wrote the songs for the 1951 film Awaara, working with Shankar-Jaikishan?
questions.explanation: Shailendra and Hasrat Jaipuri, the regular R.K. Films lyricist team, penned the songs of Awaara.

questions.q: In the backstory of the 1951 film Awaara, why does the judge Raghunath cast out his pregnant wife Leela?
options: Because he wrongly suspects the unborn child is not his after she was abducted by Jagga
Because she had committed a crime
Because she refused to leave the city
questions.explanation: After Leela is briefly abducted by the bandit Jagga, Raghunath suspects the child's paternity and throws her out, setting the tragedy in motion.

questions.q: Which actor, later a famous leading man and Raj Kapoor's younger brother, played the young Raj in the childhood portions of the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Shashi Kapoor, credited as 'Shashiraj', played the young Raj in Awaara, portraying the childhood of his elder brother Raj Kapoor's character.

questions.q: The celebrated dream sequence in the 1951 film Awaara is built around a fantastical depiction of which contrast?
questions.explanation: The lavish 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi' dream sequence visualises heaven and hell, dramatising Raj's torn inner state and considered one of Indian cinema's most ambitious sequences of its era.

questions.q: Which playback singer sang the female portions of the 1951 film Awaara's songs, including 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi'?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar provided the female vocals, including the dream-sequence song 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi'.

questions.q: The runaway popularity of the 1951 film Awaara in the Soviet Union made Raj Kapoor a household name there under which affectionate title derived from the film?
questions.explanation: The Russian-dubbed title 'Bродяга' (Bradyaga, meaning 'the vagabond/tramp') became Raj Kapoor's beloved identity in the USSR.

questions.q: Beyond the Soviet Union, in which other large country was the 1951 film Awaara also famously a huge popular success?
questions.explanation: Awaara was an enormous hit in China as well as the USSR and the Middle East, with its title song widely known there.

questions.q: Which actress played Leela, the wronged mother of the protagonist Raj, in the 1951 film Awaara?
questions.explanation: Leela Chitnis played Leela, the suffering mother cast out by the judge and forced to raise Raj in poverty.
